home.title: OpenTrade
home.tagline: A blockchain-powered review platform for financial services — KOL signals and broker reviews permanently on-chain: open, transparent, and immutable.
home.heroDescPrefix: Say goodbye to platform-controlled "black-box reviews". With Web3 technology, all live KOL signals and broker reviews are permanently written on-chain —
home.featuresSubtitle: We leverage cryptography and distributed ledger technology to solve the common problems of fraud, censorship, and information asymmetry in traditional financial review platforms.
home.featureImmutableDesc: Once a review or signal is written to the smart contract, neither broker PR teams nor the platform itself can delete or hide it.
home.featureJuryDesc: When disputes arise, a jury of randomly selected high-reputation SBT holders votes to reach a fair verdict.
home.featureKolDesc: Track KOLs with tamper-proof records. Signals are timestamped on-chain immediately — no more deleted losing trades.
home.featureZkDesc: Zero-knowledge proofs compute verification locally. You never need to expose your actual account balance to the platform.
home.faqSubtitle: Learn how OpenTrade protects your interests
home.faqQ1: Once a review is on-chain, can it really never be deleted?
home.faqA1: Yes. All reviews and KOL signals submitted by SBT-verified users are written directly to blockchain smart contracts. This means even OpenTrade administrators or broker officials cannot unilaterally tamper with or delete any historical records.
home.faqA2: SBT (Soulbound Token) is a non-transferable token. We require users to verify their broker account ownership locally using zero-knowledge proofs (zk-SNARKs), then mint an SBT upon successful verification. This ensures every review comes from a real trader while protecting user privacy.
home.faqQ3: What happens if there's a malicious review?
home.faqA3: We have a DAO decentralized jury mechanism. If a broker believes a review is purely malicious fabrication, they can initiate arbitration. Randomly selected users with no conflict of interest vote to decide the outcome, ensuring fairness.
home.ctaTitle: Ready to join the trust network?
home.ctaDescription: Whether you're an investor looking for reliable brokers or a KOL wanting to showcase real performance, this is where you belong.

footer.mission: Bringing fairness back to financial-service reviews — immutable, on chain.
footer.regulatoryNotice: This platform does not provide investment advice. All reviews are permanently stored on-chain — they cannot be deleted or tampered with.

complaintForm.pageDescription: A complaint is different from a review — it must be backed by verifiable evidence (statements, chat logs, contract screenshots). Admins review every submission within 1-2 business days and label whether the claim has been substantiated.
complaintForm.loginRequired: Submitting a complaint requires you to log in with Privy and hold the L2 verified-reviewer SBT.
complaintForm.sbtRequired: A complaint is a factual allegation against a broker, so it must be filed by a verified user. Complete the broker-statement verification flow first to mint your Reviewer SBT.
complaintForm.evidenceDropTitle: Click to select a file, or drag here
complaintForm.evidenceDropDesc: Attach the statement, contract, or support-chat screenshot that backs up your claim. You may redact sensitive asset numbers.
complaintForm.evidenceInvalidType: Unsupported file format. Please upload PDF, JPG, PNG, or WebP.
complaintForm.evidenceTooLarge: File too large. Please upload a file smaller than 10MB.
complaintForm.evidenceUploadFailed: Evidence upload failed. Please try again.
complaintForm.sentimentLabel: Your overall verdict on this broker
complaintForm.bodyPlaceholder: Describe what happened, relevant dates, amounts involved, etc. (10-2000 characters).
complaintForm.bodyHint: Stick to facts. Do not include PII (ID numbers, bank accounts) — the complaint body is pinned to IPFS and is publicly readable.
complaintForm.immutableHint: The complaint is pinned to IPFS and hashed with keccak256. Once submitted it cannot be deleted or modified.
complaintForm.submitFailed: Submission failed. Please try again in a moment.
complaintForm.successMessage: Your complaint has been recorded and is now in the admin review queue. The verification outcome will appear on the broker page's Complaints tab.
complaintForm.disclaimer: OpenTrade does not provide investment advice. Each complaint is the legal responsibility of its author; OpenTrade only provides the public, tamper-proof record layer.

steps.title: How complaints work
steps.differenceDesc: Reviews are subjective opinions; complaints are factual allegations (e.g. broker froze funds without cause, failed to execute an order). Because complaints carry higher responsibility we require evidence.
steps.evidenceDesc: The evidence file is uploaded straight to IPFS (via Pinata) — the raw file never lands in our database. Anyone can verify it independently via the CID.
steps.reviewDesc: Admins review every complaint within 1-2 business days and label whether the claim was substantiated. Even rejected complaints stay published on chain — only the platform's verification stamp is withheld.
steps.immutableDesc: Once a complaint is pinned to IPFS it cannot be deleted or modified. Stick to facts and avoid personal attacks.

wizard.guidelinesIntro: This platform provides decentralised administrative intervention mechanisms. Before submitting, please read the following rules:
wizard.guidelinesRule1: You must hold verifiable proof of your account or trading history with the broker (e.g. statement screenshots or deposit/withdrawal records).
wizard.guidelinesRule2: Malicious, fraudulent, or unsupported complaints will be rejected and may impact your account reputation.
wizard.guidelinesRule3: All submitted materials are encrypted and stored on IPFS. Only administrators can view original files.
wizard.guidelinesRule4: Complaint content is permanently recorded once submitted and cannot be deleted or modified. Ensure all statements are factual.
wizard.evidenceHint: Please provide screenshots, chat logs, or statements. This helps administrators verify your issue faster. Data will be encrypted.

steps.title: How it works
steps.step1Desc: Upload your broker statement — the file goes straight to IPFS, never stored in our DB.
steps.step2Desc: A keccak256 commitment is generated locally; the raw data cannot be recovered from it.
steps.step3Desc: An admin manually verifies your broker relationship using the IPFS evidence.
steps.step4Desc: Once approved, your Reviewer SBT is minted on-chain. Non-transferable, forever yours.

wizard.step1Subtitle: Different roles unlock different levels of platform permissions.
wizard.roleUserDesc: Verify that you hold a real brokerage account. After verification, your reviews will carry a 'Verified' badge and you may apply to join the DAO jury.
wizard.roleKolDesc: Verify account size (e.g. >$100k) to unlock the KOL dashboard and the ability to publish paid trading signals on-chain.
wizard.step2Subtitle: You selected {broker} as the verification target. The system needs to confirm your account existence.
wizard.modeApiDesc: Use a Read-Only API key for instant verification. The key will be destroyed immediately after verification and never uploaded to our servers.
wizard.modePdfDesc: Upload your latest broker statement or account screenshot. The system will extract features locally and verify authenticity.
wizard.step3Subtitle: The system is verifying your credentials locally and generating an irreversible cryptographic commitment.
wizard.step4Subtitle: Your zero-knowledge commitment will be submitted to the OpenTrade smart contract. The system will automatically mint on-chain after admin approval.
wizard.sbtWarning: Once your SBT is minted, it will be permanently bound to your currently connected wallet address and cannot be transferred. Make sure you are using your primary wallet.

verify.disclaimer: This process does not constitute account verification by the broker itself. OpenTrade verifies your relationship with the broker, not your investment decisions.

becomeAKol.heroSubtitle: OpenTrade makes every signal a financial KOL emits permanent and tamper-proof. Honest callers finally get to prove their skill with verifiable on-chain history — no more «win loudly, lose quietly».
becomeAKol.valueProp1Desc: Every signal's entry, target, and stoploss is written to Base L2 smart contracts and settled automatically against Yahoo Finance + Oracle data. No one can edit the record after the fact.
becomeAKol.valueProp2Desc: Your direction win rate, target hit rate, and per-asset-class performance are public. New followers can audit your last 90 days of real results before trusting you.
becomeAKol.valueProp3Desc: OpenTrade ships in Traditional Chinese, Simplified Chinese, and English at once. Embeddable win-rate cards drop straight into your YouTube description, Instagram bio, or Twitter profile.
becomeAKol.stateUnauthBody: Sign-up takes 30 seconds. OpenTrade supports Google, Apple, Email, crypto wallet, and iAM Smart (coming soon) — once you're in, you can apply to become a certified KOL.
becomeAKol.stateNoSbtTitle: One step left: verify your identity
becomeAKol.stateNoSbtBody: You're logged in but haven't completed L2 identity verification yet. OpenTrade requires every KOL to verify at least one broker (L2 SBT) so signal authors are confirmed real investors, not anonymous arbitrageurs.
becomeAKol.stateNoApplicationBody: You're identity-verified and haven't submitted a KOL application yet. The application is four steps: iAM Smart verification, basic profile, optional license upload, and confirm-and-submit. About 5 minutes total.
becomeAKol.statePendingBody: We've received «{name}»'s KOL application. A platform moderator will complete the review within 1-2 business days and notify you via in-app notification and email.
becomeAKol.stateApprovedBody: Congrats «{name}»! Your application is approved. You can now publish signals, monitor your win-rate dashboard, and let followers track every strategy you call.
becomeAKol.stateRejectedBody: An admin reviewed your application and decided not to approve it this time. Please adjust based on the reason below and resubmit — applicants can reapply as many times as needed.
becomeAKol.stateRejectedNoReasonFallback: No specific reason provided. Please contact support if you have questions.
becomeAKol.disclaimer: OpenTrade is pure technical infrastructure and does not provide investment advice. All KOL signals are individual trading opinions for reference only — readers must assess risk themselves. On-chain data is permanent and tamper-proof once submitted.

auth.iamSmartModalBody: We are coordinating with the HKSAR Office of the Government Chief Information Officer (OGCIO) to obtain iAM Smart login credentials. Once activated, signing in with iAM Smart will complete identity registration, Privy wallet provisioning, and KOL eligibility verification in a single step. Until then, please use one of the other login methods.

moderationAudit.title: Moderation transparency
moderationAudit.subtitle: OpenTrade's only content-moderation lever — a content-neutral pre-publication blocklist — records every change immutably. This page publishes those changes to prove the lever is never used to suppress negative reviews.
moderationAudit.intro: Moderation targets only five clearly prohibited categories (profanity, personal attacks, off-platform solicitation, illegal content, exposing a third party's private data) and never any specific broker or reviewer. To stop bad actors rephrasing around the filter, this page does not publish the blocklist itself — only each change's action, category, operator role, reason, and time.
moderationAudit.empty: No moderation changes have been recorded yet.
moderationAudit.error: Could not load the moderation history right now. Please try again later.
moderationAudit.disclaimer: This page shows only redacted moderation metadata and never publishes any blocklist content. Moderation cannot and will not be used to delete or alter on-chain reviews or to suppress negative ones.
